hypothesis: Evaluating both answer orders will remove a pure position effect, but it will not prevent false model promotion when the judge has candidate-correlated content or style bias; dropping discordant swaps will also hide the size of the unstable subset.
claimBoundary: The experiment identifies failure mechanisms under declared stochastic judge and task parameters. It does not estimate the bias of a named judge model, prove a universal human-review rate, or establish that simulated style exposure matches any production workload.

baselines: latent truth reference for every paired answer
one judgment with the candidate always presented first
one judgment with randomized presentation order
controls: position-only control removes candidate-correlated style preference
unbiased negative control removes both injected biases
all policies share the same latent truths and difficulty mix within each repeat
